clean_body: We’ve got less than 12 hours to meet our fundraising goal and I’m counting on you to get us through! We just need 9 more donors to reach our goal! Ca Friend, I’ll make this quick. We’ve got less than 12 hours to meet our fundraising goal and I’m counting on you to get us through! We just need 9 more donors to reach our goal! I can’t tell you how much it would mean to me to be able to send a bold, strong message to the dysfunctional elites that we will not tolerate their failures any longer. Let’s show Washington the Wisconsin Way by making this a successful end to the quarter and continuing strong into the rest of the year. Leah \----- Forwarded Message below \----- Friend, need a perfect example of everything wrong with Washington? Look no further than the Senate. Remember when we the people voted to repeal Obamacare? ❌ The Senate failed to make that happen. Or when we voted for a secure border? ❌ The Senate has failed to make that happen. What about larger immigration reform? ❌ Big fat NO action from the Senate there, too. And who even knows what will happen with tax reform! If we’re being honest, we don’t even expect our senators to get their jobs done at this point. This whole year has been filled with disappointing inaction and incompetence. It’s ridiculous. And it needs to change. But the only way to really change Washington is to replace our lazy politicians with some new faces. That’s why I’m running for Senate and that’s why the Washington elite will do everything they can to stop me. But Friend, with you by my side I’m confident we can defeat them. We’re just hours away from a very important fundraising deadline and I need 29 more donors in your area. Friend, this race is going to be a tough one. The status quo never likes a shake up. But a shake up is exactly what the Senate needs. Please, help me bring conservative change to the Swamp and get Washington working for you again. Donate here in the next few hours to make a huge difference: Thank you, Friend. Leah Paid for by Leah for Senate

#
Hello,

4 more hours.

That's all the time we have to knock our end-of-month deadline out of the park and take one more step towards electing Andrew as Florida's next Governor. This is a true grassroots campaign and we need your help to continue building the momentum from the ground up.

We desperately need a fresh approach to solve the problems we face and get back on track, and Andrew's the person for the job.

**[We only need 200 grassroots donors to help us reach our end-of-month deadline, so will you pitch in $3, or whatever you can afford, before midnight tonight?](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb322f/9e00690/4168804d/2403383c/1944721608/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVk1RVk5VVGtGTlJTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZhU1ZBbEpRPT0iOiIiLCJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSJ9/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gillum-eom-nov?utm_campaign=em17_171130_EOM3ND_fnd_gq_ndn__TG&utm_medium=email&utm_source=Gillum&refcode=em17_171130_EOM3ND_fnd_gq_ndn__TG&firstname=&lastname=&zip=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&utm_content=1+-+We+only+need+200+grassroots+donors+to+he")**

Andrew isn't powered by special interests or swayed by partisan politics -- he's committed to rebuilding Florida into a state that works for all of us.

Andrew has helped implement programs focused on helping former offenders and at-risk offenders from falling deeper into the criminal justice system; he has stood up to both Donald Trump and Rick Scott's policies that target immigrants, and he led the charge to expand domestic partner health benefits for Tallahassee city employees.

As Governor, Andrew will ensure Florida is a welcoming and safe place for everyone, no matter where they come from, who they love or how they identify. But in order to do that, he needs YOU. Now.

Thank you,

Team Gillum

name: The White House
subject: The Senate closes in on tax reform
body:
1600 Daily
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=4f714dde53aef105174cbef820c3363903f1e69db740fea071c3e1bb4bf9ccced6e43374b23c2a73b58be2feb64b82682d3908cdef202d47

The Senate closes in on tax reform

Today could prove to be a monumental one for the United States, as the Senate moves ever closer to passing the most ambitious tax reform bill in three decades. While Senators do the tough and necessary work of finalizing the legislation’s terms, the past 48 hours has given Americans plenty of reason to be optimistic that a tax cut will arrive in time for Christmas:

- On Wednesday, 137 economists sent an open letter to Congress backing the Tax Cuts and Jobs Act. The letter is
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=4f714dde53aef105bdf73154deb187a6a100f2a5a92710433b59819dbcfebf1f2c7c6290cd0f5e4a81f83f9a2a3d4d2927c343f098861abd
worth reading in full, but here’s the money quote: “Left virtually untouched for the last 31 years, our chart-topping corporate tax rate . . . [sent] 4,700 companies from 2004 to 2016 to cheaper shores abroad.”
- Yesterday, Senators John McCain (R-AZ) and Lisa Murkowski (R-AK) said they’ll be voting yes on the bill; this morning, Senators Ron Johnson (R-WI) and Steve Daines (R-MT) also confirmed their support, giving the legislation a major boost.
- The sense of forward momentum from Republicans on Capitol Hill is palpable. “We’re heading down the home stretch,” said Senate Majority Leader Mitch McConnell yesterday.
President Donald J. Trump stands ready to sign this historic legislation.

Read more about the President’s
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=4f714dde53aef105bdf73154deb187a6a100f2a5a92710433b59819dbcfebf1f2c7c6290cd0f5e4a81f83f9a2a3d4d2927c343f098861abd
vision to jolt economic growth.

The President delivers a Christmas message

President Trump and First Lady Melania Trump participated in the 95th annual National Christmas Tree Lighting last night. Visitors gathered on the Ellipse at President’s Park to listen to musical acts including the Beach Boys, Mannheim Steamroller, and more.

Speaking at the ceremony, the President underscored the religious significance of Christmas even as he spoke about the holiday’s universal truths. “Whatever our beliefs, we know that the birth of Jesus Christ and the story of his life forever changed the course of human history,” the President said. “There is hardly an aspect of our lives today that his life has not touched.”

Watch video of the President’s remarks
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=4f714dde53aef10509306bd71a690b21b2cd69feaa69bb14fb23da6dd02b74981f76d485e38524ffe5f578baf43690b43e44f9cbc5c65eb6
here.

President Trump hosts Libyan Prime Minister

President Trump met with Libyan Prime Minister Fayez al-Sarraj at the White House today, capping off a busy week that included in-person or phone conversations between the U.S. President and world leaders from China, South Korea, and Bahrain. Yesterday, President Trump spoke with President Moon Jae-in of the Republic of Korea for the second time since North Korea launched an intercontinental ballistic missile on November 28.

While several of this week’s conversations were sparked by that missile test, today the President turned his attention to North Africa. He and the Libyan Prime Minister discussed ways to cooperate on counterterrorism and expand bilateral engagement between the two countries.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=4f714dde53aef105aa3dc15b619d4b47aaa70926b3b16eaa3b46efa95b3aa9d9e03a6c923e5ab9003a8cb5378454c87cd7d9b0d0e90ecff8
Read the White House statement on today’s meeting.

Photo of the Day

President Donald J. Trump and First Lady Melania Trump participate in the 2017 National Christmas Tree Lighting | November 30, 2017 (Official White House Photo by Joyce N. Boghosian)

POTUS TODAY

President Trump met with the Prime Minister of Libya before having lunch with Secretary of State Rex Tillerson and Secretary of Defense James Mattis. The President will meet with Secretary of the Treasury Steven Mnuchin this afternoon.

COMING UP

Be on the lookout for the President’s Weekly Address, coming out tomorrow. This week’s edition will commemorate American civil rights icon Rosa Parks, who made history when she refused to give up her bus seat to a white passenger 62 years ago today.

clean_body: 1600 Daily The Senate closes in on tax reform Today could prove to be a monumental one for the United States, as the Senate moves ever closer to passing the most ambitious tax reform bill in three decades. While Senators do the tough and necessary work of finalizing the legislation’s terms, the past 48 hours has given Americans plenty of reason to be optimistic that a tax cut will arrive in time for Christmas: - On Wednesday, 137 economists sent an open letter to Congress backing the Tax Cuts and Jobs Act. The letter is worth reading in full, but here’s the money quote: “Left virtually untouched for the last 31 years, our chart-topping corporate tax rate . . . [sent] 4,700 companies from 2004 to 2016 to cheaper shores abroad.” - Yesterday, Senators John McCain (R-AZ) and Lisa Murkowski (R-AK) said they’ll be voting yes on the bill; this morning, Senators Ron Johnson (R-WI) and Steve Daines (R-MT) also confirmed their support, giving the legislation a major boost. - The sense of forward momentum from Republicans on Capitol Hill is palpable. “We’re heading down the home stretch,” said Senate Majority Leader Mitch McConnell yesterday. President Donald J. Trump stands ready to sign this historic legislation. Read more about the President’s vision to jolt economic growth. The President delivers a Christmas message President Trump and First Lady Melania Trump participated in the 95th annual National Christmas Tree Lighting last night. Visitors gathered on the Ellipse at President’s Park to listen to musical acts including the Beach Boys, Mannheim Steamroller, and more. Speaking at the ceremony, the President underscored the religious significance of Christmas even as he spoke about the holiday’s universal truths. “Whatever our beliefs, we know that the birth of Jesus Christ and the story of his life forever changed the course of human history,” the President said. “There is hardly an aspect of our lives today that his life has not touched.” Watch video of the President’s remarks here. President Trump hosts Libyan Prime Minister President Trump met with Libyan Prime Minister Fayez al-Sarraj at the White House today, capping off a busy week that included in-person or phone conversations between the U.S. President and world leaders from China, South Korea, and Bahrain. Yesterday, President Trump spoke with President Moon Jae-in of the Republic of Korea for the second time since North Korea launched an intercontinental ballistic missile on November 28. While several of this week’s conversations were sparked by that missile test, today the President turned his attention to North Africa. He and the Libyan Prime Minister discussed ways to cooperate on counterterrorism and expand bilateral engagement between the two countries. Read the White House statement on today’s meeting. Photo of the Day President Donald J. Trump and First Lady Melania Trump participate in the 2017 National Christmas Tree Lighting | November 30, 2017 (Official White House Photo by Joyce N. Boghosian) POTUS TODAY President Trump met with the Prime Minister of Libya before having lunch with Secretary of State Rex Tillerson and Secretary of Defense James Mattis. The President will meet with Secretary of the Treasury Steven Mnuchin this afternoon. COMING UP Be on the lookout for the President’s Weekly Address, coming out tomorrow. This week’s edition will commemorate American civil rights icon Rosa Parks, who made history when she refused to give up her bus seat to a white passenger 62 years ago today.

name: Jason Yates, CEO of My Faith Votes
subject: Tell President Trump you support moving the US Embassy to Jerusalem
body:
| | Tell President Trump you support moving the US Embassy to Jerusalem
---
[ ](http://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.myfaithvotes.org%2F&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=171201_p2a_s1&n=1)
| Dear Peter, I want to ask you to be in prayer for President Trump as he considers making a historic decision this weekend. In 1980, Israel passed a law declaring Jerusalem the united capital of Israel. In support, the U.S. Congress passed a law in 1995 mandating the U.S. embassy be moved from Tel Aviv to Jerusalem. However, since the passage of the law, every President – Clinton, Bush, and Obama – has declined to move the embassy, issuing a waiver every six months to delay the move The six-month deadline for the waiver is again approaching, and the Trump administration has said they are engaged in serious discussions to move the U.S. embassy to Jerusalem instead of again signing a six-month waiver. **This would send a bold and important message to the world of America's unwavering support of Israel in the midst of heightened international tension**. Vice President Pence gave an exclusive interview to CBN's David Brody. “For the past twenty years, Congress and successive administrations have expressed a willingness to move our American embassy but as I announced earlier this week, President Trump is actively considering moving the American embassy from Tel Aviv to Jerusalem. It’s a very serious matter. It’s one that he’s taking very seriously to heart but the President and I believe that the relationship between the American people and the people of Israel is one that is timeless." - Vice President Pence **Take a minute to use our simple interactive tool to send****a message to the White House and President Trump letting him know you support moving the U.S. embassy to Jerusalem and fulfilling the 1995 law.** | | [Tell the President you support the move! ►](http://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=http%3A%2F%2Fp2a.co%2FwIMhqBX&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=171201_p2a_s1&n=2)
---
May this be our prayer. **The statutory deadline for President Trump to make the decision is Monday, December 4.**
* * *
“May peace be within your walls, and prosperity within your palaces. For the sake of my brothers and my friends, I will now say, may peace be within you. For the sake of the house of the LORD our God, I will seek your good.” – Psalm 122:7
**United. We Stand.**

Jason Yates
CEO
[](http://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.myfaithvotes.org%2F&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=171201_p2a_s1&n=3)
| [Take Action Now! ►](http://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=http%3A%2F%2Fp2a.co%2FwIMhqBX&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=171201_p2a_s1&n=4)

**NH REPUBLICAN PARTY****WEEKLY NEWSLETTER****FRIDAY, DECEMBER 1ST** **_A Note From Jeanie_** **Dear Derek,**
**This past week I had the opportunity to participate in the House Caucus to elect the new Speaker. It was a great experience and I appreciated how professional and well-organized House Clerk Paul Smith was in putting the process together.********The five candidates, Reps. Al Baldasaro, John Burt, Gene Chandler, Laurie Sanborn, and Steve Smith gave thoughtful speeches on why they wanted the job. The consistent theme throughout was unity. As a NH Republican and Chair of the NHGOP, I especially appreciated that theme. ********Representative Chandler won on the second ballot and on Thursday of this week was confirmed as the new Speaker of the House. I am looking forward to working with Speaker Chandler, Senate President Morse, and Governor Sununu to continue pushing a thoughtful, conservative agenda in 2018 that protects our hardworking taxpayers.********Republican leadership will also be unified in making sure we hold and grow our majorities. We know that 2018 will not be a “cake walk” for Republicans because the Democrats are angry and energized. Not only did they lose the Oval Office, but they also lost the Corner Office and are still in the minority in the NH House and Senate. The Democrats are also angry because NH Republicans are making a real difference—[because we are working together and moving New Hampshire’s economy in the right direction.](http://email.robly.com/wf/click?upn=gT2AZ1ZnBy-2FrecDREa9-2BbY1qiyT3HuRDH6pVWPAnYR4QGW9sVxJWjnybZ4rwaVNhE9Mf3NVpRqSDZb1Dcrg2HymWPiZwfxa8CHskfCskgpk-3D_enNSH-2FxHAAqXgxiHRFihpX91d75ERAkpfg4XQfmYhCFb47pW8iDRAYLsM9sf9wIa92fG-2FsxU68zXzzMA2nO2VCunl2bm-2FhLXsWS8P3XITs28GUU7Mnlu37POPaWv9myv4YWBbFU9plZi0-2B42tZfBaUujH4mxA3e6jOiyDa5q6HKz45-2B17g6DUNL-2BnIMAR30MoBMAUtF31R1Kf1m6XjSkxD-2F8nQgAMyOHUrxbu-2B-2BDXzygtPdIAwZQYVfgLNRJQWqtUF2Dc5G-2FS4etJYLMMOJaCjT1C7bxTGYH0tY-2BK-2BPONAJb4SXZBWmFbPWpVRJLfTJoekGS9vuwG-2BP5-2FLay-2BBP7Har3Q4WZYfuSqOfeOLPkh8cB6JOYWujVXDPCo-2FBw7us8NRi4EK4frsEL8bElWS7CZ6a5tWXyF93dEgeuy54tH1rY-2B2cSZGeLcU-2F38UicX2IHHhulp56kwxO3NQexb00R8A-3D-3D) In fact, 75% of the folks in New Hampshire believe we are headed in the right direction!********With January just around the corner, we are laying the foundation to recruit strong candidates for the House and Senate. If you haven’t thought about running for office, I encourage you to consider it. If you know of someone who would make a good candidate, encourage them to run.********NH Republicans have the track record and the right agenda to keep New Hampshire moving forward. That, coupled with a unified Republican Party, will ensure success in 2018.**
**Sincerely, **
**Jeanie** **Join the Grassroots Circle!**

**The Grassroots Circle is a unique way for you to get involved while supporting the Party.**
**Becoming a member couldn't be easier -- join today for as little as $10 per month. There are three levels of support: The Grassroots Supporter, the NHGOP Advocate and the VIP Investor.** [ **More Information** ](http://email.robly.com/wf/click?upn=5MdO6cLza2MazVJVOHyurnZ5zJfDwV5-2F8Hl4xre-2BIMt5nd0pHim8EqEA9pV9ZCkm_enNSH-2FxHAAqXgxiHRFihpX91d75ERAkpfg4XQfmYhCFb47pW8iDRAYLsM9sf9wIa92fG-2FsxU68zXzzMA2nO2VCunl2bm-2FhLXsWS8P3XITs28GUU7Mnlu37POPaWv9myv4YWBbFU9plZi0-2B42tZfBaUujH4mxA3e6jOiyDa5q6HKz45-2B17g6DUNL-2BnIMAR30MoBMAUtF31R1Kf1m6XjSkxD-2F8nQgAMyOHUrxbu-2B-2BDXzygtPdIAwZQYVfgLNRJQWqtUF2Dc5G-2FS4etJYLMMOJaCkExK07MOF-2BCKS8enW4jiPERnyTHPAh7MzHKdBXp-2BSKt-2BEvlrbiMghZtDuNDljC4eY3KexfONwi-2BMjsorxwWvlkXmeWA6rOfahOY7B4Hi6I-2FtDV0pYEbCEZkbRQUYcaZyl17gUxxqfWSJmG6gird8UCHRDDAUlfHPbI-2B8cApfEg4yPRWtq8Bfyzo-2BkEpaHvqKg-3D-3D) **Intern with the NH GOP!**

**Are you a student or know a student that is interested in Politics?******
**Learn the skills necessary to win elections and meet the Republican leaders that govern our State and write legislation.** [ **More Information** ](http://email.robly.com/wf/click?upn=5MdO6cLza2MazVJVOHyurv1j0CcSmOfr0T-2BdH8AHkRpn0Des-2Fk7zqP5dVsN4j6jJ_enNSH-2FxHAAqXgxiHRFihpX91d75ERAkpfg4XQfmYhCFb47pW8iDRAYLsM9sf9wIa92fG-2FsxU68zXzzMA2nO2VCunl2bm-2FhLXsWS8P3XITs28GUU7Mnlu37POPaWv9myv4YWBbFU9plZi0-2B42tZfBaUujH4mxA3e6jOiyDa5q6HKz45-2B17g6DUNL-2BnIMAR30MoBMAUtF31R1Kf1m6XjSkxD-2F8nQgAMyOHUrxbu-2B-2BDXzygtPdIAwZQYVfgLNRJQWqtUF2Dc5G-2FS4etJYLMMOJaCg7noqNTuL13I4UDQ6T3TMw10TO5x2Kvp6Pq-2B6ep0DrFW0VWYktpmRLv5kG7nuzSiBd4Pr7nsDlL3oaKPrEz1zV6iIaw1SEO1aAtteGn71xgr-2BP0a5HcqunQHQsD3hgoNwv4X0temV1UT-2BfbAiktekppw3eAjCElbA97YTSkCIumceS6wEFfK3pKF4rsZGr4Uw-3D-3D) **FEATURED EVENT** **SRW Annual Holiday Luncheon**
**December 8 @ 11:30 am - 1:30 pm**
**Annual Holiday Luncheon Guest Speaker, Tiffany Eddy**
**Social Hour 11:30-12:30 pm (cash bar)**
**12:30 pm lunch, start of program**
**Price is $45.00 pp, check can be mailed to SRW, PO Box 495, Greenland, NH.**
**Any questions please can Donna Slack at 603-433-9850.**

clean_body: nh.gop NH REPUBLICAN PARTYWEEKLY NEWSLETTERFRIDAY, DECEMBER 1ST A Note From Jeanie Dear Derek, This past week I had the opportunity to participate in the House Caucus to elect the new Speaker. It was a great experience and I appreciated how professional and well-organized House Clerk Paul Smith was in putting the process together.****The five candidates, Reps. Al Baldasaro, John Burt, Gene Chandler, Laurie Sanborn, and Steve Smith gave thoughtful speeches on why they wanted the job. The consistent theme throughout was unity. As a NH Republican and Chair of the NHGOP, I especially appreciated that theme. ****Representative Chandler won on the second ballot and on Thursday of this week was confirmed as the new Speaker of the House. I am looking forward to working with Speaker Chandler, Senate President Morse, and Governor Sununu to continue pushing a thoughtful, conservative agenda in 2018 that protects our hardworking taxpayers.****Republican leadership will also be unified in making sure we hold and grow our majorities. We know that 2018 will not be a “cake walk” for Republicans because the Democrats are angry and energized. Not only did they lose the Oval Office, but they also lost the Corner Office and are still in the minority in the NH House and Senate. The Democrats are also angry because NH Republicans are making a real difference— In fact, 75% of the folks in New Hampshire believe we are headed in the right direction!****With January just around the corner, we are laying the foundation to recruit strong candidates for the House and Senate. If you haven’t thought about running for office, I encourage you to consider it. If you know of someone who would make a good candidate, encourage them to run.****NH Republicans have the track record and the right agenda to keep New Hampshire moving forward. That, coupled with a unified Republican Party, will ensure success in 2018. Sincerely, Jeanie Join the Grassroots Circle! The Grassroots Circle is a unique way for you to get involved while supporting the Party. Becoming a member couldn't be easier -- join today for as little as $10 per month. There are three levels of support: The Grassroots Supporter, the NHGOP Advocate and the VIP Investor. Intern with the NH GOP! Are you a student or know a student that is interested in Politics?** Learn the skills necessary to win elections and meet the Republican leaders that govern our State and write legislation. FEATURED EVENT SRW Annual Holiday Luncheon December 8 @ 11:30 am - 1:30 pm Annual Holiday Luncheon Guest Speaker, Tiffany Eddy Social Hour 11:30-12:30 pm (cash bar) 12:30 pm lunch, start of program Price is $45.00 pp, check can be mailed to SRW, PO Box 495, Greenland, NH. Any questions please can Donna Slack at 603-433-9850. NH GOP Christmas Care Package Drive Between now and December 2nd, the NH GOP will collecting items for a coordinated Christmas Care Package Drive with the Republican National Committee. PHOTO OF THE WEEK The unified candidates for NH House Speaker supporting Gene Chandler following the GOP Caucus on Tuesday! REPUBLICAN SPOTLIGHT Thank you for all of your submissions! This week's Republican spotlight is for State Representative David Testerman, R-Franklin! Representative Testerman has been awarded the honorary rank of major by the New Hampshire Civil Air Patrol, the official civilian auxiliary of the U.S. Air Force. Who do you think should be in the spotlight in next week's newsletter? Team,

We've got a big fight ahead on the GOP tax scam.

This plan is a giant tax giveaway for giant corporations that are already making record profits. It's just one more round of tilting the playing field for the wealthiest Americans -- at the expense of the middle class.

We need to know you're standing with Senate Democrats in this fight -- and there's no better time to show your support.

[**Pitch in $1 to prove to the GOP you 're standing behind Senate Democrats 100% -- in this fight and all the important fights ahead.**](http://action.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/dscc-ewarren?refcode=DPE-20171201-Warren-ProsHV&recurring=auto&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1= &city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=")

This fight is about numbers, but it's also about values.

Not one middle-class family in America should have to pay more in taxes so that billionaires can get a special break. Period.

But right now, GOP mega-donors are threatening to withhold hundreds of millions of dollars in campaign donations to Republicans if they don't deliver massive tax giveaways. That's why Mitch McConnell and the Senate Republicans are desperate to get this scheme through the Senate.

Here's the thing: When Republicans tried to repeal Obamacare, Senate Democrats didn't have the votes on our own to stop them. But while we held the line, supporters like you got engaged in the fight -- and together, we saved health care for millions of Americans.

Now, we need people like you across the country to step up again and fight back -- the same way we defeated their "health care" bill just a few months ago.

name: =?utf-8?Q?Eric=20Greitens?=
subject: Kids come first.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Greitens for Missouri
body:
\------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Peter,
Today, kids, teachers, and families won. The State Board of Education voted for new leadership for our school system. That's a major step in the right direction as we work to improve public education in Missouri.

We need to: raise teacher pay, support public schools, and help students succeed. We need to make sure that the money Missourians spend on schools gets out of the bureaucracy--and into the classroom.

Our teachers need a raise. If they just got paid at the national average, they'd make nearly $10,000 more a year. Meanwhile, we've got more administrators than most of the country, and their pay is rising more than twice as fast as teacher pay. Some make big bucks--more than $250,000 a year--while too many teachers struggle to get by.

And from 2009 to 2015, Missouri fell from 18th to 28th in fourth-grade reading and from 23rd to 32nd in eighth-grade math. According to ACT testing, three out of every four kids who graduate from Missouri high schools aren't ready for college.

These problems have gone on too long. We're demanding better. Because our teachers deserve it. Because our students deserve it.

I support public education. We added $64,000,000 in the budget for public schools. More money than ever before is being spent on education. We fully funded the system for the first time in years.

The bureaucrats took your money. Teachers didn't get a raise. Juniors in high school had the ACT cut.

The bureaucrats had their chance. They failed our kids.

Defenders of the status quo have been nasty. They harass, call names, and intimidate. Many of them have big salaries that they don't want to lose, and records they don't want to be held accountable for.

Well, today things have changed: Kids come first.

Thank you,
Eric Greitens
Paid for by Greitens for Missouri
Jeff Stuerman, Treasurer
This email was sent to dpwillis67+greitens@gmail.com. You can also follow us on [Facebook](https://ericgreitens.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=c81357bdb59fb170d9eb82b3a&id=9b2da48c9a&e=84041254e6) and [Twitter](https://ericgreitens.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=c81357bdb59fb170d9eb82b3a&id=1da6b6ab3e&e=84041254e6). name: Emily Cain, EMILY's List
subject: That "Election Day 2017" feeling 😎
body:
We elect pro-choice Democratic women who will fight for women and families. | [ ](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c6efb/24c69fd9/15a3fde6b/189162f5/3358722802/VEsH/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/donate-to-emily?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=1+-+%0D%0A++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++&utm_campaign=WQEFEL02N_digital_email_20171201_Runners22k_inact&source=WQEFEL02N_digital_email_20171201_Runners22k_inact")
---

Peter,

We're still feeling the amazing aftereffects of Election Day 2017.

In the two weeks following November 7th, nearly twice as many women reached out to us than had in the entire 2016 cycle.

Our successes across the country sent a clear message to women: Even if you are a first-time candidate, even if you don't have a lengthy political career, even if there isn't anyone who looks like you serving in your local government -- you too can run for office and win.

But just like our women who won this year, these hundreds of new prospective candidates can't win without help.

**[Donate $3 to train and support these women to run for state and local office-- and we can elect a new generation of women lawmakers.](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c6efb/24c69fd9/15a3fde6b/189162f6/3358722802/VEsE/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20171201_email_Runners22k?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=2+-+Donate+3+to+train+and+support+these+wome&utm_campaign=WQEFEL02N_digital_email_20171201_Runners22k_inact&source=WQEFEL02N_digital_email_20171201_Runners22k_inact")**

Now, a total of more than 22,000 women have told us they want to put their name on the ballot.

This year, we have already trained more than 2,000 women to run for office. Next year, we are going to continue helping women get ready to run, but we are also going to start launching campaigns for women, too.

And just like we did this year, we're going to endorse women early and often so they make it through competitive primaries, ones where they're often running against the infamous old boys' club.

Trust me: The women are here, they are ready to run, and they CAN win. We just need to support them.

**Donate $3 to help train and elect women who want to run for office for the first time:**

**[https://secure.emilyslist.org/Support-Women-Running](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c6efb/24c69fd9/15a3fde6b/189162f7/3358722802/VEsF/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20171201_email_Runners22k?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=3+-+httpssecureemilyslistorgSupportWomenRunn&utm_campaign=WQEFEL02N_digital_email_20171201_Runners22k_inact&source=WQEFEL02N_digital_email_20171201_Runners22k_inact")**

Thanks, and go team!

Emily Cain
Executive Director, EMILY's List
